behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the `git -> CLI parser/categorizer` boundary (rename detection vs add+delete split), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename classification depends on real git diff/status semantics and rename thresholds from a real repo/subprocess; mocked git output can hide wrong flags or parsing assumptions. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: feed canned diff text with `R100 old -> new`, assert only `renamed=1`; catches parser bugs but misses wrong git invocation that never emits rename records. System candidate: create temp repo, commit file, rename it, run real CLI, assert renamed count increments and add/delete do not; catches real invocation/format mismatches. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely git/CLI counting with no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report has the file only in `renamed` and not also in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk comes from real subprocess output/encoding and binary diff markers crossing module boundaries. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: binary handling failures often appear only with real git output (e.g., non-text content, `Binary files differ`, decode paths), which stubs may sanitize and miss. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: pass fixture diff text containing binary marker, assert count increments; catches simple parser logic but not real decode/subprocess edge failures. System candidate: create binary file in repo, commit/change it, run CLI subprocess, assert exit code success and binary count present; catches real crash modes. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output quality or multi-agent pipeline exists in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI process, assert it exits successfully and the report includes the binary file in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is triggered by real git repository state (`unborn HEAD`) and subprocess error behavior, not isolated function logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked git calls can force expected outputs and miss real exit codes/messages for no-commit repos. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: mock git layer to return empty changes, assert empty report; misses handling of real `git` failure path. System candidate: run CLI in freshly `git init` repo with no commits, assert successful execution and empty report; catches incorrect subprocess error handling. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior only concerns git state/error handling, no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert stdout is an empty structured report and process exit is non-error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates end-to-end correctness of history extraction plus categorization across modules. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: correctness depends on real git history traversal and revision semantics via subprocess; stubs can mirror assumptions and mask drift. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: feed synthetic change events and assert aggregate counts; catches arithmetic errors but misses wrong git revision range/query behavior. System candidate: build real commit sequence with add/delete/rename/modify, run CLI, assert report counts match known ground truth; catches real history-query defects. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text focusing on deterministic git history counts with no model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary repository with a known sequence of commits covering add/delete/rename/modify, run CLI on that repo, and assert each category count equals the constructed ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure depends on git index vs working-tree semantics at real command boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: staged/unstaged equivalence can fail due to real git flags and index state that in-process mocks usually flatten. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: simulate two inputs (staged-like/unstaged-like) to service, assert same output; misses incorrect git command selection/options. System candidate: in real repo, create same content change once unstaged and once staged, run CLI each time, assert byte-identical stdout; catches index/worktree handling defects. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is deterministic CLI/git state comparison without AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real git repo, produce equivalent file changes in unstaged state and staged state in separate runs, execute the CLI both times, and assert reports are identical.
